name: Research + Write
description: Mirrors the toddly plan-first research+write pattern: clarify goal → web research (parallel topics) → synthesise findings → draft document → HITL quality gate → write final file. Maps LLMExecutor tool loop to agent_role nodes, MARK_AWAITING_USER to hitl_breakpoint, and parallel_group metadata to fork/join.

goal.description: User-supplied research goal (maps to toddly goal node description)

clarifications.description: Answers to clarification fields (maps to toddly clarification node result)

research_queries.description: Sub-topics to research in parallel (maps to parallel_group tasks)

raw_findings.description: Raw search results per sub-topic (append reducer — mirrors toddly SET_RESULT per node)

reviewer_feedback.description: Human reviewer decision (maps to HITL resume payload)

final_document.description: Polished final document (maps to write_file execution step)

web_search.description: Maps to toddly web_research skill — search_web execution_type

fetch_url.description: Maps to toddly fetch_url execution_type in LLMExecutor tool loop

agents.backstory: Expert at finding accurate, up-to-date information via web search and URL fetching.
agents.goal: Gather comprehensive, source-attributed findings on an assigned sub-topic.

agents.backstory: Turns raw multi-source findings into coherent, de-duplicated summaries.
agents.goal: Produce a structured synthesis from all parallel research branches.

agents.backstory: Transforms structured analysis into polished, well-structured documents.
agents.goal: Write a clear, well-structured document matching the user goal and addressing reviewer feedback.

nodes.label: Clarify goal
nodes.prompt:
Before planning, I need a few details to scope the research correctly.

Goal: {{$.state.goal}}

Please answer:
1. Target audience (e.g. executive summary, technical deep-dive, blog post)
2. Desired output format (e.g. markdown report, bullet summary, long-form article)
3. Any sources to prefer or exclude
4. Approximate length (e.g. 500 words, 2000 words)

nodes.label: Plan research sub-topics
nodes.system_prompt: You are the planning engine of an autonomous task-execution system. Your job is to decompose a research goal into 2–4 concrete sub-topics that can be researched in parallel. Respond with JSON only — no explanation, no markdown.

config.expected_output: At least 3 key findings with source URLs. One finding per paragraph.
At least 3 key findings with source URLs. One finding per paragraph.
At least 3 key findings with source URLs. One finding per paragraph.
A structured synthesis: key themes, supporting evidence, source URLs. 400–600 words.
A complete first draft in the requested format, addressing the goal and audience.

nodes.label: Quality review

config.expected_output: Revised document incorporating all reviewer feedback.
